Backend Programming Notes for BE Role

Focus: High Traffic, Concurrency, Lock Patterns, and Practical Implementation

Table of Contents

  1. High Traffic Patterns
    • Idempotent Request Processing
    • Rate Limiting
    • Circuit Breaker
    • Request Deduplication with Distributed Lock
  2. Concurrency Patterns
    • ConcurrentHashMap Operations
    • Atomic Operations
    • Lock Strategies
  3. API Design Patterns
  4. Java Design Patterns for Backend

High Traffic Patterns

1. Idempotent Request Processing

Problem: Design a mechanism that prevents duplicate processing of requests with the same requestId within a configurable time window.

Initial Design (V0 - Interview Whiteboard)

1/**
2 * Design a mechanism that prevents duplicate processing of requests
3 * with the same requestId within a configurable time window
4 *
5 * Key considerations:
6 * - Is duplicate? -> check requestId
7 * - Configurable time window
8 * - Thread safety
9 * - Lock timeout
10 */
11
12int TIME_WINDOW = 10;
13int LOCK_TIME_OUT = 60; // 60 sec
14
15class Request {
16    String reqId;
17    String msg;
18    long timestamp; // unix time
19}
20
21class Resp {
22    String status;
23}
24
25public class IdempotentRequestProcessor {
26
27    Map<String, Request> processedReq;
28
29    IdempotentRequestProcessor() {
30        this.map = new HashMap();  // NOT thread-safe!
31    }
32
33    public Resp processRequest(Request req) {
34        // Problem: Race condition here!
35        if (this.map.contains(req.reqId)) {
36            if (map.get(req.reqId).timestamp < req.time - TIME_WINDOW) {
37                return new Resp("duplicated req in time window");
38            }
39        }
40
41        this.put(req.reqId, req);
42        return new Resp("req is received");
43    }
44}

Issues with V0:

  1. HashMap is NOT thread-safe
  2. Check-then-act is NOT atomic (race condition)
  3. No cleanup mechanism for expired entries
  4. Lock timeout not implemented

Solution V1: ConcurrentHashMap with Atomic Check

1import lombok.AllArgsConstructor;
2import lombok.Data;
3import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;
4import java.util.Map;
5import java.time.Instant;
6
7@Data
8@AllArgsConstructor
9class Request {
10    private String reqId;
11    private String msg;
12    private long timestamp; // Unix timestamp in seconds
13}
14
15@Data
16@AllArgsConstructor
17class Resp {
18    private String status;
19    private String message;
20}
21
22public class IdempotentRequestProcessor {
23
24    // Configurable window in seconds
25    private static final int TIME_WINDOW = 10;
26
27    // Stores RequestId -> Timestamp of first successful receipt
28    private final Map<String, Long> processedReqs = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
29
30    public Resp processRequest(Request req) {
31        long currentTime = Instant.now().getEpochSecond();
32
33        // Check if duplicate within window
34        Long existingTimestamp = processedReqs.get(req.getReqId());
35
36        if (existingTimestamp != null) {
37            if (currentTime - existingTimestamp < TIME_WINDOW) {
38                return new Resp("REJECTED", "Duplicate request within time window.");
39            }
40            // Outside window - treat as fresh request
41        }
42
43        // Store the request timestamp
44        processedReqs.put(req.getReqId(), currentTime);
45
46        return executeBusinessLogic(req);
47    }
48
49    private Resp executeBusinessLogic(Request req) {
50        try {
51            // Simulate processing
52            return new Resp("SUCCESS", "Request processed: " + req.getReqId());
53        } catch (Exception e) {
54            // If fails, remove from map so user can retry
55            processedReqs.remove(req.getReqId());
56            return new Resp("ERROR", "Processing failed.");
57        }
58    }
59
60    // TODO: Add cleanup task (ScheduledExecutor) to prevent infinite growth
61}

Improvement but still has issue:

  • get() then put() is NOT atomic - still has race condition!

Solution V2: Using compute() for True Atomicity

1import java.util.concurrent.*;
2
3class Request {
4    String reqId;
5    String msg;
6    long timestamp; // epoch seconds
7
8    public Request(String reqId, String msg, long timestamp) {
9        this.reqId = reqId;
10        this.msg = msg;
11        this.timestamp = timestamp;
12    }
13}
14
15class Resp {
16    String status;
17
18    public Resp(String status) {
19        this.status = status;
20    }
21}
22
23public class IdempotentRequestProcessor {
24
25    private final long TIME_WINDOW_SECONDS = 10;
26
27    // reqId -> last processed timestamp
28    private final ConcurrentHashMap<String, Long> processedReq = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
29
30    public Resp processRequest(Request req) {
31        long now = req.timestamp;
32
33        // Atomic check + update using compute()
34        Long[] resultHolder = new Long[1];
35        boolean[] isDuplicate = new boolean[1];
36
37        processedReq.compute(req.reqId, (key, oldTimestamp) -> {
38            if (oldTimestamp == null) {
39                // First time seeing this request
40                resultHolder[0] = now;
41                isDuplicate[0] = false;
42                return now;
43            }
44
45            if (now - oldTimestamp <= TIME_WINDOW_SECONDS) {
46                // Duplicate within window - keep old timestamp
47                isDuplicate[0] = true;
48                return oldTimestamp;
49            }
50
51            // Outside window - update timestamp
52            resultHolder[0] = now;
53            isDuplicate[0] = false;
54            return now;
55        });
56
57        if (isDuplicate[0]) {
58            return new Resp("duplicate request");
59        }
60
61        // Process actual request
62        return process(req);
63    }
64
65    private Resp process(Request req) {
66        try {
67            // Simulate business logic
68            return new Resp("processed: " + req.reqId);
69        } catch (Exception e) {
70            return new Resp("failed");
71        }
72    }
73}

Solution V3: Production-Ready with Cleanup

1import java.util.concurrent.*;
2import java.time.Instant;
3
4public class IdempotentRequestProcessor {
5
6    private final long timeWindowSeconds;
7    private final ConcurrentHashMap<String, Long> processedReqs = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
8    private final ScheduledExecutorService cleanupExecutor;
9
10    public IdempotentRequestProcessor(long timeWindowSeconds, long cleanupIntervalSeconds) {
11        this.timeWindowSeconds = timeWindowSeconds;
12
13        // Schedule periodic cleanup to prevent memory leak
14        this.cleanupExecutor = Executors.newSingleThreadScheduledExecutor();
15        cleanupExecutor.scheduleAtFixedRate(
16            this::cleanupExpiredEntries,
17            cleanupIntervalSeconds,
18            cleanupIntervalSeconds,
19            TimeUnit.SECONDS
20        );
21    }
22
23    public Resp processRequest(Request req) {
24        long now = Instant.now().getEpochSecond();
25
26        boolean[] isDuplicate = {false};
27
28        processedReqs.compute(req.getReqId(), (key, oldTimestamp) -> {
29            if (oldTimestamp == null) {
30                return now;
31            }
32            if (now - oldTimestamp <= timeWindowSeconds) {
33                isDuplicate[0] = true;
34                return oldTimestamp;
35            }
36            return now;
37        });
38
39        if (isDuplicate[0]) {
40            return new Resp("REJECTED", "Duplicate request");
41        }
42
43        return executeBusinessLogic(req);
44    }
45
46    private void cleanupExpiredEntries() {
47        long now = Instant.now().getEpochSecond();
48        processedReqs.entrySet().removeIf(
49            entry -> now - entry.getValue() > timeWindowSeconds * 2
50        );
51    }
52
53    public void shutdown() {
54        cleanupExecutor.shutdown();
55    }
56
57    private Resp executeBusinessLogic(Request req) {
58        // Business logic here
59        return new Resp("SUCCESS", "Processed: " + req.getReqId());
60    }
61}

Solution V4: Distributed Version with Redis

1import redis.clients.jedis.Jedis;
2import redis.clients.jedis.params.SetParams;
3
4public class DistributedIdempotentProcessor {
5
6    private final Jedis redis;
7    private final long timeWindowSeconds;
8    private static final String KEY_PREFIX = "idempotent:";
9
10    public DistributedIdempotentProcessor(Jedis redis, long timeWindowSeconds) {
11        this.redis = redis;
12        this.timeWindowSeconds = timeWindowSeconds;
13    }
14
15    public Resp processRequest(Request req) {
16        String key = KEY_PREFIX + req.getReqId();
17
18        // SETNX with expiration - atomic operation
19        String result = redis.set(
20            key,
21            String.valueOf(System.currentTimeMillis()),
22            SetParams.setParams().nx().ex(timeWindowSeconds)
23        );
24
25        if (result == null) {
26            // Key already exists - duplicate request
27            return new Resp("REJECTED", "Duplicate request");
28        }
29
30        // First time - process the request
31        try {
32            return executeBusinessLogic(req);
33        } catch (Exception e) {
34            // On failure, delete key to allow retry
35            redis.del(key);
36            return new Resp("ERROR", "Processing failed");
37        }
38    }
39
40    private Resp executeBusinessLogic(Request req) {
41        return new Resp("SUCCESS", "Processed: " + req.getReqId());
42    }
43}

Key Takeaways for Idempotent Processing

Aspect Single Instance Distributed
Storage ConcurrentHashMap Redis / Database
Atomicity compute() / computeIfAbsent() SETNX with TTL
Cleanup ScheduledExecutorService Redis TTL auto-expiry
Failure Handling Remove from map on error Delete key on error

2. Rate Limiting

Token Bucket Implementation

1import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicLong;
2import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReference;
3
4public class TokenBucketRateLimiter {
5
6    private final long maxTokens;
7    private final long refillRate; // tokens per second
8    private final AtomicReference<State> state;
9
10    private static class State {
11        final long tokens;
12        final long lastRefillTime;
13
14        State(long tokens, long lastRefillTime) {
15            this.tokens = tokens;
16            this.lastRefillTime = lastRefillTime;
17        }
18    }
19
20    public TokenBucketRateLimiter(long maxTokens, long refillRate) {
21        this.maxTokens = maxTokens;
22        this.refillRate = refillRate;
23        this.state = new AtomicReference<>(new State(maxTokens, System.nanoTime()));
24    }
25
26    public boolean tryAcquire() {
27        while (true) {
28            State currentState = state.get();
29            long now = System.nanoTime();
30            long elapsedNanos = now - currentState.lastRefillTime;
31
32            // Calculate tokens to add
33            long tokensToAdd = (elapsedNanos * refillRate) / 1_000_000_000L;
34            long newTokens = Math.min(maxTokens, currentState.tokens + tokensToAdd);
35
36            if (newTokens < 1) {
37                return false; // No tokens available
38            }
39
40            // Try to consume one token
41            State newState = new State(newTokens - 1, now);
42            if (state.compareAndSet(currentState, newState)) {
43                return true;
44            }
45            // CAS failed - retry
46        }
47    }
48}

Sliding Window Rate Limiter

1import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentLinkedDeque;
2import java.util.Deque;
3
4public class SlidingWindowRateLimiter {
5
6    private final int maxRequests;
7    private final long windowSizeMs;
8    private final Deque<Long> requestTimestamps = new ConcurrentLinkedDeque<>();
9
10    public SlidingWindowRateLimiter(int maxRequests, long windowSizeMs) {
11        this.maxRequests = maxRequests;
12        this.windowSizeMs = windowSizeMs;
13    }
14
15    public synchronized boolean tryAcquire() {
16        long now = System.currentTimeMillis();
17        long windowStart = now - windowSizeMs;
18
19        // Remove expired timestamps
20        while (!requestTimestamps.isEmpty() && requestTimestamps.peekFirst() < windowStart) {
21            requestTimestamps.pollFirst();
22        }
23
24        if (requestTimestamps.size() < maxRequests) {
25            requestTimestamps.addLast(now);
26            return true;
27        }
28
29        return false;
30    }
31}

3. Circuit Breaker

1import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicInteger;
2import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicLong;
3
4public class CircuitBreaker {
5
6    private enum State { CLOSED, OPEN, HALF_OPEN }
7
8    private volatile State state = State.CLOSED;
9    private final AtomicInteger failureCount = new AtomicInteger(0);
10    private final AtomicLong lastFailureTime = new AtomicLong(0);
11
12    private final int failureThreshold;
13    private final long resetTimeoutMs;
14    private final int halfOpenMaxCalls;
15    private final AtomicInteger halfOpenCalls = new AtomicInteger(0);
16
17    public CircuitBreaker(int failureThreshold, long resetTimeoutMs, int halfOpenMaxCalls) {
18        this.failureThreshold = failureThreshold;
19        this.resetTimeoutMs = resetTimeoutMs;
20        this.halfOpenMaxCalls = halfOpenMaxCalls;
21    }
22
23    public boolean allowRequest() {
24        switch (state) {
25            case CLOSED:
26                return true;
27
28            case OPEN:
29                if (System.currentTimeMillis() - lastFailureTime.get() > resetTimeoutMs) {
30                    state = State.HALF_OPEN;
31                    halfOpenCalls.set(0);
32                    return true;
33                }
34                return false;
35
36            case HALF_OPEN:
37                return halfOpenCalls.incrementAndGet() <= halfOpenMaxCalls;
38
39            default:
40                return false;
41        }
42    }
43
44    public void recordSuccess() {
45        if (state == State.HALF_OPEN) {
46            state = State.CLOSED;
47        }
48        failureCount.set(0);
49    }
50
51    public void recordFailure() {
52        lastFailureTime.set(System.currentTimeMillis());
53
54        if (state == State.HALF_OPEN) {
55            state = State.OPEN;
56            return;
57        }
58
59        if (failureCount.incrementAndGet() >= failureThreshold) {
60            state = State.OPEN;
61        }
62    }
63
64    public <T> T execute(SupplierWithException<T> action) throws Exception {
65        if (!allowRequest()) {
66            throw new CircuitBreakerOpenException("Circuit breaker is OPEN");
67        }
68
69        try {
70            T result = action.get();
71            recordSuccess();
72            return result;
73        } catch (Exception e) {
74            recordFailure();
75            throw e;
76        }
77    }
78
79    @FunctionalInterface
80    public interface SupplierWithException<T> {
81        T get() throws Exception;
82    }
83
84    public static class CircuitBreakerOpenException extends RuntimeException {
85        public CircuitBreakerOpenException(String message) {
86            super(message);
87        }
88    }
89}

4. Request Deduplication with Distributed Lock

1import redis.clients.jedis.Jedis;
2import redis.clients.jedis.params.SetParams;
3import java.util.UUID;
4
5public class DistributedLockWithDedup {
6
7    private final Jedis redis;
8    private final String lockPrefix = "lock:";
9    private final String processedPrefix = "processed:";
10
11    public DistributedLockWithDedup(Jedis redis) {
12        this.redis = redis;
13    }
14
15    /**
16     * Process request with distributed lock and deduplication
17     */
18    public <T> T processWithLock(String requestId, long lockTimeoutSec,
19                                  long dedupWindowSec, RequestProcessor<T> processor) {
20
21        // 1. Check if already processed
22        if (redis.exists(processedPrefix + requestId)) {
23            throw new DuplicateRequestException("Request already processed");
24        }
25
26        // 2. Acquire distributed lock
27        String lockKey = lockPrefix + requestId;
28        String lockValue = UUID.randomUUID().toString();
29
30        String acquired = redis.set(
31            lockKey, lockValue,
32            SetParams.setParams().nx().ex(lockTimeoutSec)
33        );
34
35        if (acquired == null) {
36            throw new LockNotAcquiredException("Could not acquire lock");
37        }
38
39        try {
40            // 3. Double-check after acquiring lock
41            if (redis.exists(processedPrefix + requestId)) {
42                throw new DuplicateRequestException("Request already processed");
43            }
44
45            // 4. Process the request
46            T result = processor.process();
47
48            // 5. Mark as processed
49            redis.setex(processedPrefix + requestId, dedupWindowSec, "1");
50
51            return result;
52
53        } finally {
54            // 6. Release lock (only if we own it)
55            releaseLock(lockKey, lockValue);
56        }
57    }
58
59    /**
60     * Lua script for safe lock release (only release if we own the lock)
61     */
62    private void releaseLock(String lockKey, String lockValue) {
63        String script =
64            "if redis.call('get', KEYS[1]) == ARGV[1] then " +
65            "    return redis.call('del', KEYS[1]) " +
66            "else " +
67            "    return 0 " +
68            "end";
69
70        redis.eval(script,
71            java.util.Collections.singletonList(lockKey),
72            java.util.Collections.singletonList(lockValue)
73        );
74    }
75
76    @FunctionalInterface
77    public interface RequestProcessor<T> {
78        T process();
79    }
80
81    public static class DuplicateRequestException extends RuntimeException {
82        public DuplicateRequestException(String message) { super(message); }
83    }
84
85    public static class LockNotAcquiredException extends RuntimeException {
86        public LockNotAcquiredException(String message) { super(message); }
87    }
88}

Concurrency Patterns

ConcurrentHashMap Operations

Method Behavior Use Case
putIfAbsent(k, v) Insert only if key absent Simple dedup
computeIfAbsent(k, func) Compute value lazily if absent Lazy initialization
compute(k, func) Always compute (atomic read-modify-write) Conditional updates
merge(k, v, func) Merge with existing value Counters, aggregation 
1// Example: Atomic counter increment
2ConcurrentHashMap<String, AtomicLong> counters = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
3
4// Safe increment
5counters.computeIfAbsent("hits", k -> new AtomicLong(0)).incrementAndGet();
6
7// Conditional update
8counters.compute("hits", (k, v) -> {
9    if (v == null) return new AtomicLong(1);
10    v.incrementAndGet();
11    return v;
12});

Atomic Operations

1import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
2
3// AtomicInteger/Long for simple counters
4AtomicLong counter = new AtomicLong(0);
5counter.incrementAndGet();
6counter.compareAndSet(expected, newValue);
7
8// AtomicReference for object references
9AtomicReference<State> stateRef = new AtomicReference<>(initialState);
10stateRef.compareAndSet(oldState, newState);
11
12// LongAdder for high-contention counters (better than AtomicLong)
13LongAdder adder = new LongAdder();
14adder.increment();
15adder.sum(); // Get total

Lock Strategies

1. ReentrantLock with Timeout

1import java.util.concurrent.locks.ReentrantLock;
2import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
3
4ReentrantLock lock = new ReentrantLock();
5
6public void processWithTimeout() throws InterruptedException {
7    if (lock.tryLock(5, TimeUnit.SECONDS)) {
8        try {
9            // Critical section
10        } finally {
11            lock.unlock();
12        }
13    } else {
14        throw new RuntimeException("Could not acquire lock");
15    }
16}

2. ReadWriteLock for Read-Heavy Workloads

1import java.util.concurrent.locks.ReadWriteLock;
2import java.util.concurrent.locks.ReentrantReadWriteLock;
3
4ReadWriteLock rwLock = new ReentrantReadWriteLock();
5
6public Data read() {
7    rwLock.readLock().lock();
8    try {
9        return data;
10    } finally {
11        rwLock.readLock().unlock();
12    }
13}
14
15public void write(Data newData) {
16    rwLock.writeLock().lock();
17    try {
18        this.data = newData;
19    } finally {
20        rwLock.writeLock().unlock();
21    }
22}

3. StampedLock for Optimistic Read

1import java.util.concurrent.locks.StampedLock;
2
3StampedLock lock = new StampedLock();
4
5public Data optimisticRead() {
6    long stamp = lock.tryOptimisticRead();
7    Data localData = this.data;
8
9    // Check if data was modified during read
10    if (!lock.validate(stamp)) {
11        // Fall back to regular read lock
12        stamp = lock.readLock();
13        try {
14            localData = this.data;
15        } finally {
16            lock.unlockRead(stamp);
17        }
18    }
19    return localData;
20}

API Design Patterns

Idempotency Key Pattern

1@PostMapping("/orders")
2public ResponseEntity<Order> createOrder(
3    @RequestHeader("Idempotency-Key") String idempotencyKey,
4    @RequestBody CreateOrderRequest request) {
5
6    // Check if already processed
7    Optional<Order> existing = orderService.findByIdempotencyKey(idempotencyKey);
8    if (existing.isPresent()) {
9        return ResponseEntity.ok(existing.get());
10    }
11
12    // Process and store with idempotency key
13    Order order = orderService.createOrder(request, idempotencyKey);
14    return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(order);
15}

Retry with Exponential Backoff

1public <T> T executeWithRetry(Supplier<T> action, int maxRetries) {
2    int attempt = 0;
3    long backoffMs = 100;
4
5    while (true) {
6        try {
7            return action.get();
8        } catch (Exception e) {
9            attempt++;
10            if (attempt >= maxRetries) {
11                throw e;
12            }
13
14            try {
15                Thread.sleep(backoffMs + (long)(Math.random() * backoffMs));
16                backoffMs *= 2; // Exponential backoff
17            } catch (InterruptedException ie) {
18                Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
19                throw new RuntimeException(ie);
20            }
21        }
22    }
23}

Java Design Patterns for Backend

1. Singleton (Thread-Safe)

1// Double-checked locking with volatile
2public class Singleton {
3    private static volatile Singleton instance;
4
5    private Singleton() {}
6
7    public static Singleton getInstance() {
8        if (instance == null) {
9            synchronized (Singleton.class) {
10                if (instance == null) {
11                    instance = new Singleton();
12                }
13            }
14        }
15        return instance;
16    }
17}
18
19// Better: Enum Singleton
20public enum SingletonEnum {
21    INSTANCE;
22
23    public void doSomething() { }
24}
25
26// Better: Static holder (lazy + thread-safe)
27public class SingletonHolder {
28    private SingletonHolder() {}
29
30    private static class Holder {
31        static final SingletonHolder INSTANCE = new SingletonHolder();
32    }
33
34    public static SingletonHolder getInstance() {
35        return Holder.INSTANCE;
36    }
37}

2. Producer-Consumer with BlockingQueue

1import java.util.concurrent.*;
2
3public class ProducerConsumer {
4
5    private final BlockingQueue<Task> queue = new LinkedBlockingQueue<>(100);
6    private final ExecutorService consumers = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(4);
7
8    public void start() {
9        for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
10            consumers.submit(() -> {
11                while (!Thread.currentThread().isInterrupted()) {
12                    try {
13                        Task task = queue.take(); // Blocks if empty
14                        task.process();
15                    } catch (InterruptedException e) {
16                        Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
17                        break;
18                    }
19                }
20            });
21        }
22    }
23
24    public void submit(Task task) throws InterruptedException {
25        queue.put(task); // Blocks if full
26    }
27
28    public void shutdown() {
29        consumers.shutdownNow();
30    }
31}

3. Object Pool Pattern

1import java.util.concurrent.BlockingQueue;
2import java.util.concurrent.LinkedBlockingQueue;
3
4public class ConnectionPool {
5
6    private final BlockingQueue<Connection> pool;
7    private final int maxSize;
8
9    public ConnectionPool(int maxSize) {
10        this.maxSize = maxSize;
11        this.pool = new LinkedBlockingQueue<>(maxSize);
12
13        // Pre-create connections
14        for (int i = 0; i < maxSize; i++) {
15            pool.offer(createConnection());
16        }
17    }
18
19    public Connection acquire() throws InterruptedException {
20        return pool.take();
21    }
22
23    public void release(Connection conn) {
24        if (conn != null && !conn.isClosed()) {
25            pool.offer(conn);
26        } else {
27            // Replace bad connection
28            pool.offer(createConnection());
29        }
30    }
31
32    private Connection createConnection() {
33        // Create new connection
34        return new Connection();
35    }
36}

Common Interview Questions

Q1: Why use ConcurrentHashMap.compute() instead of get+put?

Answer: get() followed by put() is NOT atomic. Between these two operations, another thread could modify the map. compute() performs the entire read-modify-write as a single atomic operation.

Q2: Difference between synchronized and ReentrantLock?

Feature synchronized ReentrantLock
Lock timeout No Yes (tryLock)
Interruptible No Yes
Fairness No Configurable
Multiple conditions No Yes
Try lock No Yes

Q3: When to use volatile?

Use volatile when:

  • Variable is accessed by multiple threads
  • Only simple read/write operations (no read-modify-write)
  • No compound operations needed

NOT sufficient for: counter++ (read-modify-write needs AtomicInteger)

Q4: How to prevent deadlock?

  1. Lock ordering: Always acquire locks in the same order
  2. Lock timeout: Use tryLock() with timeout
  3. Avoid nested locks: Minimize lock scope
  4. Use higher-level constructs: ConcurrentHashMap, BlockingQueue

Summary: Choosing the Right Tool

Scenario Solution
Simple counter AtomicLong or LongAdder
Conditional map update ConcurrentHashMap.compute()
Read-heavy cache ReadWriteLock or StampedLock
Request deduplication (single node) ConcurrentHashMap + TTL cleanup
Request deduplication (distributed) Redis SETNX with TTL
Rate limiting Token Bucket / Sliding Window
Fault tolerance Circuit Breaker
Producer-Consumer BlockingQueue

References

  • Java Concurrency Guide
  • Redis Distributed Locks
  • Martin Fowler - Circuit Breaker
